scope_boundary: Human oversight degradation finite fixture only. The fixture validates three bounded synthetic records and rejects seven expected-invalid controls for missing reviewer qualification, fatigued approval, rubber-stamping, automation-bias contradiction, alarm fatigue, support-promotion overclaim, and missing non-claim boundaries. It does not run a deployed approval workflow, measure reviewer correctness, measure alert quality, conduct a human-subjects study, prove approval-service behavior, or promote the Runtime Adapters chapter core claim.
transition_reason: The human-oversight degradation fixture is useful because it makes reviewer fatigue, rubber-stamping, alarm fatigue, automation bias, reviewer qualification, independent-evidence checks, and non-claim boundaries executable as finite adapter-review routes. It remains a synthetic record fixture, not deployed approval-workflow evidence, so it blocks promotion for approval-service quality, reviewer correctness, deployed human-factors behavior, safety, runtime-adapter behavior, or chapter-core support.
required_artifacts: public-safe deployed or externally reviewable approval-workflow trace
reviewer-load fields from a real or externally reviewable process
independent-evidence-check records tied to concrete review decisions
reviewer-rotation or delay behavior under overload
alert-quality or false-alarm evidence with residuals
accepted evidence-transition record for any narrowed upward support movement
negative_results: fatigued reviewer approval routes to rotation or delay
automation-bias contradiction without independent evidence is blocked
support promotion from human approval shape is rejected
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: fatigued approval accepted as ordinary approval
support-state promotion without a separate accepted upward transition
fixture described as deployed approval-workflow evidence
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed human-oversight degradation or approval-workflow claim after public-safe deployed or externally reviewable workflow traces, reviewer-load fields, independent-evidence checks, reviewer rotation or delay behavior, alert-quality evidence, privacy-boundary handling, residuals, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted upward transition.
limitations: does not promote any chapter core claim
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent human-factors, approval-workflow, runtime-adapter, safety, source-interpretation, or external audit
acceptance_blockers: no public-safe deployed or externally reviewable approval-workflow trace
no real reviewer-load or alert-quality evidence
non_claims: does not promote human oversight degradation evidence above argument
does not prove approval-service quality, reviewer correctness, deployed human-factors behavior, runtime-adapter safety, alert quality, deployment safety, or ASI
does not create an upward support-state transition
does not promote any chapter core claim
does not promote the Runtime Adapters chapter core claim
